headline: Solar PPA prices buck trends, increasing in Europe and falling in North America, in Q2 2026
summary_80w: The average price of a solar PPA signed in Europe in the second quarter of 2026 reached US$74.85/MWh, according to LevelTen Energy.

headline: Aquila Clean Energy energises 38MW solar PV plant in New Zealand’s Bay of Plenty
summary_80w: Aquila Clean Energy APAC has energised the 38MW Omeheu solar PV power plant in Edgecumbe, situated in New Zealand's Bay of Plenty region.

headline: Australia suspends 21 solar companies from Small-scale Renewable Energy Scheme in compliance crackdown
summary_80w: Australia's CER has suspended 21 companies from the Small-scale Renewable Energy Scheme (SRES) during the April to June 2026 quarter.

headline: Australia’s Clean Energy Investor Group calls for faster, clearer EPBC Act implementation
summary_80w: The Clean Energy Investor Group (CEIG) has published a report calling on the Australian government to prioritise consistent and timely implementation of the reformed Environment Protection and Biodiversity Conservation (EPBC) Act.

headline: China to end tax breaks on certain PV cells, battery products
summary_80w: After over a decade, China is revising its long-running consumption tax exemption policy for PV cells and a range of battery products.

headline: Naturgy begins construction on 330MW Fraser Coast solar-plus-storage project in Queensland, Australia
summary_80w: Naturgy's Australian generation subsidiary Global Power Generation (GPG) has entered the construction phase of the 330MW Fraser Coast Hybrid Project in Queensland.

headline: Maine submits plans for first competitive energy storage solicitation, eyeing 360MW
summary_80w: The Maine, US Department of Energy Resources (DOER) announced plans for the state's first competitive energy storage solicitation up to 360MW.

headline: South Carolina court upholds zoning decision against Silfab Solar manufacturing facility
summary_80w: A South Carolina circuit court has upheld a decision by the York County Board of Zoning Appeals (BZA) that solar panel manufacturing is not a permitted activity in the county’s Light Industrial zoning district, a setback for Silfab Solar.

headline: Oxford PV, Caelux join global AI venture to advance materials discovery
summary_80w: Perovskite innovators Oxford PV and Caelux are part of a new AI-enabled research network set up to accelerate breakthroughs in materials science.

headline: Developer rPlus Energies commissions 125MW Idaho solar PV project
summary_80w: US renewables developer rPlus Energies has started commercial operations at its 125MW Pleasant Valley Solar 2 project in Ada County, Idaho.

headline: Comstock, Illuminate USA ink module recycling agreement
summary_80w: Recycling solution firm Comstock Metals and module manufacturer Illuminate USA have signed a solar material recycling services agreement.

headline: India updates solar cell ALMM List-II, adds Avaada Electro
summary_80w: MNRE's eighth ALMM List-II revision expands Fujiyama Power's PV cell capacity and adds Avaada Electro as a new entrant.

headline: Kyrgyzstan eyes 2GW solar cell plant with Singapore’s Sunvera Solar
summary_80w: The government of Kyrgyzstan is in discussions with Sunvera Solar over a solar cell manufacturing plant with a planned capacity of up to 2GW.

headline: Iberdrola reports US$4.9 billion H1 2026 net profit as solar capacity increases, but renewable generation falls
summary_80w: Iberdrola has reported net profits of US$4.9 billion in the first half of 2026, following its divestment from its Mexican operations.

headline: Hawaiian Electric launches RFP seeking renewables on three islands
summary_80w: Utility Hawaiian Electric Company has launched a request for proposals (RFP) seeking renewables and energy storage across three islands.

headline: Yanara secures €150 million from Mirova to develop 2GW+ renewable energy portfolio across Australia
summary_80w: Yanara has secured a €150 million investment from Mirova to develop more than 2GW of multi-tech renewable energy projects across Australia.

headline: Frontier Energy appoints Monford as EPC contractor for 132MW solar-plus-storage project in Western Australia
summary_80w: Frontier Energy has appointed Monford Group EPC contractor for the 132MWdc stage one of its Waroona Renewable Energy Project.

headline: VIDEO: Integrating solar, storage, grid-forming and AI with Envision Energy
summary_80w: We caught up with Michael Koller, Solutions Director for Europe and the UK with Envision Energy, at this year’s Smarter E Europe trade show in Munich.

headline: 1.5GWh+ of BESS deals in Italy: Sonnedix, Strioga buy projects; BNZ, RWE order from Sungrow and LONGi
summary_80w: Activity in Italy's grid-scale market is accelerating, with Sonnedix and the Strioga Family Foundation acquiring projects totalling over 1GWh combined, while BNZ and RWE have ordered BESS from Sungrow and LONGi respectively totalling 540MWh.

headline: British Columbia and BC Hydro advance first ‘major’ BESS in Canadian province
summary_80w: Canadian electric utility BC Hydro and the province of British Columbia (BC) are advancing the first major battery energy storage system (BESS) project in the province.

headline: SINEXCEL on engineering grid-forming PCS for European utility-scale BESS projects
summary_80w: SINEXCEL’s Luke Lu discusses grid-forming power conversion systems, noise constraints and O&M as the company builds its presence in Europe.

headline: Energy Storage Awards 2026: Judges seek winners with the ambition to solve industry problems
summary_80w: With the Energy Storage Awards 2026 entry deadline less than two months away, we speak to four judges to hear what they are looking for from successful entrants.

headline: Northern Europe: Delta Capacity adds to Nordic BESS portfolio, Ignitis takes FID on Latvia project
summary_80w: Delta Capacity has brought online the Nordic region’s biggest project to date and has acquired another project that would be almost twice the size.

headline: VIDEO: ‘Not enough priority given to BESS asset management and operation’
summary_80w: BESS deployments in Europe are growing exponentially year-on-year, but is asset management, operations & maintenance (O&M) and data given enough priority? A data executive from BayWa r.e. discussed the topic.

headline: Samsung C&T submits 1,000MWh solar-plus-storage project in New South Wales to Australia’s EPBC Act
summary_80w: Samsung C&T Renewable Energy Australia has submitted the 1GWh Boro solar-plus-storage project in New South Wales to Australia's EPBC Act.

headline: Wärtsilä completes delivery of 1,038 Quantum units to 1,400MWh Wooreen BESS in Australia
summary_80w: All 1,038 Wärtsilä Quantum battery units have been delivered and installed at the 1.4GWh Wooreen BESS in Victoria, Australia.

headline: Return and Engie, EnBW and Zelestra agree long-term BESS tolls in Spain, Italy
summary_80w: Return and Engie have agreed a long-term BESS toll in Spain while EnBW and Zelestra have finalised one in Italy, totalling 55MW and 300MW of capacity respectively.

headline: India awards long-duration energy storage contracts for 1,344MW pumped hydro, utility-scale flow battery
summary_80w: Two big developments for India’s emerging long-duration energy storage (LDES) sector, with contracts awarded for pumped hydro energy storage (PHES) and the country’s first large-scale flow battery.

headline: Site adaptation is the non-negotiable standard for utility-scale solar
summary_80w: Site adaptation has become the backbone of solar project finance and modern technical designs, according to Solargis' Martin Opatovsky.

headline: Perovskites are moving beyond ‘demonstration era’, says Sofab Inks as it raises US$6 million in seed funding
summary_80w: Sofab Inks has today announced that it has secured US$6 million in to expand its tin-based ETL solution for use in perovskite solar cells.

headline: Nextpower completes Prevalon acquisition
summary_80w: Solar PV solutions provider Nextpower has completed the acquisition of battery energy storage system (BESS) integrator Prevalon Energy.

headline: Statkraft makes FID in 131MW European solar PV as it reduces net loss in Q2 2026
summary_80w: Norwegian IPP Statkraft has made final investment decisions (FID) in two solar PV projects in Ireland and the UK, combining 131MWp, in the second quarter of 2026.

headline: 2.5GW Brazil gas bill could increase curtailment by 12% – Aurora Energy Research
summary_80w: A Brazilian government bill mandating 2.5GW of new gas-fired power plants will increase the curtailment of renewable energy plants by 12%, according to analysis from Aurora Energy Research.

headline: Avantus starts commercial operations at Aratina 1 solar-plus-storage project in California
summary_80w: Avantus has started commercial operations at its Aratina 1 project in California, which combines 200MW of solar PV and 500MWh of batteries.

headline: Top Chinese PV players’ combined deficit tops RMB10 billion in H1 2026
summary_80w: Listed Chinese PV companies have taken a heavy hit from persistent overcapacity across the industry chain in the first half of 2026, as reflected in their interim performance forecasts.

headline: Strong domestic market and Middle East demand drive growth in Indian solar EPC and O&M work
summary_80w: 'Home market strength plus Middle Eastern demand' has driven a surge in solar PV capacity in India, according to RenewAtlas and Wiki-Solar.

headline: Ember: EU hybridisation could unlock 25GW solar PV and wind without new grids
summary_80w: PV Tech Premium speaks with Ember's Elisabeth Cremona on the 25GW of hydropower hybridisation potential across seven EU countries.

headline: ‘Set it and forget it’: California BESS operators miss out on millions with static bidding strategies, Gridmatic says
summary_80w: Energy-Storage.news Premium speaks with David Miller, CCO of Gridmatic, about the company’s latest report from CAISO.

headline: Spearmint Energy receives permit for 600MWh Minnesota BESS
summary_80w: Battery storage developer-operator Spearmint Energy has received a site permit for the 150MW/600MWh Midwater Energy Storage Project, in Freeborn County, Minnesota, US.

headline: FEOC compliance for BESS is a decade-long obligation
summary_80w: Intertek CEA's Daniel Finn-Foley and Paul Wormser dive into the new world US energy storage developers are navigating since the introduction of FEOC restrictions.

headline: Romania ROUNDUP: Aukera, ENEVO Group and Kraftfeld Energy, and PPC Renewables advance BESS projects
summary_80w: Energy companies are accelerating battery storage deployment in Romania, with Aukera, ENEVO Group and Kraftfeld Energy, and PPC Renewables Romania announcing significant project developments.

headline: European Energy reaches financial close on 80MWh Mokoan solar-plus-storage site in Australia
summary_80w: Danish developer European Energy has reached financial close on the 80MWh Mokoan solar-plus-storage project in Victoria, Australia.

headline: South Australia’s Lunio Energy brings 20MWh grid-scale battery storage system online
summary_80w: South Australia battery storage developer Lunio Energy has energised its first grid-scale BESS at Strathalbyn and started trading in the NEM.

headline: JA begins module shipments for 5.2GW 24/7 Abu Dhabi project, Trina eyes 1.5GW Yemen PV pipeline
summary_80w: JA has begun shipping modules under a multi-gigawatt order bound for a 5.2GW “round-the-clock” solar-plus-storage project in Abu Dhabi.

headline: 1.8GWh Germany BESS moves to construction; 15GWh+ progressed since grid fees decision
summary_80w: The shareholders of the 900MW Waltrop BESS project in Germany have approved key infrastructure works for the project, with full construction scheduled for the next few months.

headline: Australia achieves record renewables and storage connections with 9.1GW reaching full output in FY26
summary_80w: Australia’s National Electricity Market (NEM) connected 9.1GW of new generation and energy storage to full output in FY26.

headline: US DOC starts AD/CVD probe into Ethiopian solar cell producers, Toyo claims it ‘does not use Chinese wafers’
summary_80w: The US Department of Commerce (DOC) has begun an antidumping and countervailing duty (AD/CVD) investigation into solar cells produced in Ethiopia.

headline: Malaysia launches 2.5GW solar-plus-storage tender
summary_80w: The government of Malaysia has unveiled the next round of its Large-scale solar (LSS) program tender seeking 2.5GW solar PV co-located with 1.25GW battery energy storage system (BESS).

headline: China’s new standards for PV production set to cut outdated technology across value chain – PV Tech Research
summary_80w: China’s new set of rules, unveiled last week, are expected to “do a good job in cutting out low-cost, outdated technology across the value chain”, according to a report from PV Tech Research.

headline: MNRE extends ALMM List-II exemption for net-metering and open access solar projects

headline: EU plans to make Europe ‘the world’s first electro-continent’ through Electrification Action Plan
summary_80w: The European Commission has released its final Electrification Action Plan to “make Europe the world’s first ‘electro-continent’.”

headline: Lodestone Energy begins work on 31.5MWp solar PV plant in New Zealand

headline: Residential BESS ‘access without upfront cash’: Palmetto launches battery subscription in 25 US states
summary_80w: Residential renewable energy solutions company Palmetto recently launched its Palmetto Energy Backup Plan, a residential battery subscription aimed at making residential storage more accessible in 25 US states.

headline: Masdar reaches financial close on ‘round-the-clock’ hybrid solar project with 19GWh BESS in UAE
summary_80w: UAE state-owned IPP Masdar has reached financial close on what it has claimed is the world’s “first gigascale 24/7 renewable energy project”.

headline: EU releases Electrification Action Plan, reaffirms 200GW 2030 energy storage target

headline: Middle East, Southeast Asia and India emerge as key alternate manufacturing hubs to China
summary_80w: As the battery storage market continues its strong growth trajectory, battery manufacturers are ramping up production to meet increased demand.

headline: Hithium looks to replicate residential solution ARKVOLT’s Asian success into Europe
summary_80w: Hithium, an integrated energy storage solutions provider known for its leadership in long-duration energy storage, is now seeing strong momentum across its residential businesses and C&I. We discussed this with the company at the Smarter E Europe trade show in Germany last month.

headline: CHINT: Bridging AI infrastructure and energy transition through integrated power solutions
summary_80w: CHINT Global's Beibei Zheng discusses how the company is responding to Europe's dual challenge of AI sovereignty and energy security.

headline: Australia adds 9.1GW of new generation and storage in FY26 with battery storage dominating
summary_80w: Australia's NEM connected 9.1GW of new generation and energy storage to full output in FY26, more than double the result achieved in FY25.

headline: Australia moves to legislate net-generator requirement for large-scale data centres
summary_80w: Australian Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has said that large-scale data centres will be legally required to put at least as much energy into the grid as they draw from it, and that this supply must be renewable energy.

headline: Zambia signs contracts for 312MW constituency solar programme
summary_80w: The Zambian government has signed five contractor groups to build 312MW of solar capacity, with a 2MW solar plant in each constituency.

headline: Sol Systems reaches financial close on 123MW Illinois solar portfolio
summary_80w: US solar developer Sol Systems has reached financial close on its 123MWac Peoria Solar Portfolio in Illinois.

headline: The summer solar surge is here for many. The challenge now is not scale, but flexibility
summary_80w: Renewable energy generation is now accelerating faster than energy systems can absorb it, according to Schneider Electric's Frédéric Godemel.

headline: Qcells secures ‘first’ UL, IEC certifications for perovskite tandem tech
summary_80w: Qcells has become the first company to achieve UL Standards & Engagement (UL) and the International Electrotechnical Commission (IEC) certifications for silicon-perovskite tandem solar technology.

headline: Reaching the ‘ready-to-repower’ stage of solar PV project maintenance
summary_80w: PV Talk: Solclaris' Joe Miletic discusses the 'ready-to-repower' stage of PV project O&M and how it differs from the 'ready-to-build' stage.

headline: SMA Solar increases 2026 guidance due to ‘improved market conditions’
summary_80w: German solar inverter producer SMA Solar has raised its full-year 2026 financial guidance following its preliminary Q2 financial results.

headline: Unirac acquires Terrasmart solar racking business

headline: Array Technologies acquires wires, cables and BoS specialist Affordable Wire Management
summary_80w: Array Technologies has entered into a definitive agreement to acquire wire management, cable protection and balance of system specialist Affordable Wire Management.

headline: Suntech: Building Trust Through 25 Years of Solar Innovation
summary_80w: At Intersolar 2026, PV Tech sat down with Suntech's General Manager Mr. Yang Hao to discuss how a quarter-century of experience, combined with robust industrial backing, positions the company for the industry's next chapter.

headline: California batteries missing out on revenue due to suboptimal bidding strategies, Gridmatic analysis finds
summary_80w: Battery energy storage systems (BESS) operating in California's grid left approximately US$98 million on the table due to suboptimal bidding strategies, according to a new analysis from Gridmatic.

headline: Eos to provide energy storage for US Department of Defense ‘Golden Dome,’ releases preliminary Q2 financials
summary_80w: Zinc hybrid cathode battery and storage system maker Eos Energy Enterprises has announced a strategic partnership with the US Department of Defense (DoD) to “enhance the resilience of national defence infrastructure.”

headline: ‘It’s on the up now’: How grid fee clarity unlocked BW ESS’ German ambitions
summary_80w: BW ESS' executive director Roberto Jimenez discusses the company's recently-announced first Germany project, in Klostermansfeld, Saxony-Anhalt, which could be one of Europe's largest when completed in 2028.

headline: Waaree, India’s biggest PV module supplier, opens 5.15GWh battery storage assembly plant
summary_80w: A subsidiary of Indian renewable energy equipment and component manufacturer Waaree Energies has opened a battery energy storage system (BESS) enclosure factory.

summary_80w: German solar and storage developer ib vogt has received state planning approval for the 120MW/480MWh Wagga North battery energy storage system (BESS) in New South Wales, Australia.

summary_80w: The Port of Newcastle has become the first port in New South Wales, Australia, to be approved to safely store grid-scale lithium-ion battery storage systems.

headline: Alpex Solar seeks ALMM inclusion as 2.2GW cell plant nears production
summary_80w: Alpex Solar expects commercial production to begin in August 2026 at its 2.2 GW TOPCon G12R solar cell facility in Kosi Kotwan, Mathura, UP.

summary_80w: LONGi’s has unveiled a suite of new technologies intended to demonstrate how the PV industry can innovate its way out of its current malaise.

headline: NextEra, Dominion submit merger applications to US regulators

headline: LONGi reports 35.5% efficiency for silicon-perovskite tandem solar cell
summary_80w: LONGi's crystalline silicon-perovskite tandem solar cell has achieved a power conversion efficiency of 35.5%.

headline: ISC Konstanz appoints Dr Gerhard Mathiak as head of module department
summary_80w: German solar energy research institute ISC Konstanz has appointed Dr Gerhard Mathiak as its head of its module department.

headline: SSI awards first traceability certification to Chinese polysilicon plant
summary_80w: The first Chinese polysilicon manufacturer has secured a certification from the Solar Stewardship Initiative’s (SSI) supply chain traceability standard.

headline: Oman finances 6GW solar cell, 3GW solar module manufacturing facility
summary_80w: Oman has announced US$1.7 billion for 105 projects and investments across a number of sectors, including solar cell and module manufacturing.

headline: ‘We’ll be producing heterojunction this year in the US,’ says SEG Solar CEO
summary_80w: Jim Wood, SEG Solar's CEO, explains the company's US manufacturing roadmap and why it chose heterojunction technology for its new facilities.

headline: Navigating US solar cell supply: a framework for a fractured market

headline: Sunrun, FranklinWH expand BESS virtual power plant capacity in California, Texas
summary_80w: Distributed energy resource (DER)-focused companies Sunrun and FranklinWH are expanding in California and Texas, US, respectively.

headline: Alfen and CATL agree 5GWh sodium-ion BESS partnership in Europe
summary_80w: System integrator Alfen and battery company CATL have struck a 5GWh partnership for sodium-ion BESS deployments in Europe.

headline: Thailand needs ‘many regulatory reforms’ of electricity sector to catch up with ASEAN leaders on energy storage
summary_80w: Electricity sector regulations in Thailand require significant reforms to support the use of energy storage as a critical tool for renewable energy integration.

headline: EU ROUNDUP: Operators commission 682MWh of BESS in Germany, Estonia, Belgium, Denmark and Bulgaria

headline: Behind CATL’s ‘zero degradation’ BESS technology
summary_80w: We analyse recent details that have emerged on battery OEM CATL's zero-degradation BESS technology, which was first announced two years ago.

headline: Hithium on the European C&I BESS market and its need for a 4-hour solution
summary_80w: A shift is happening in Europe’s commercial & industrial (C&I) energy storage market, and integrated energy storage solutions provider Hithium is addressing this demand with a purpose-built 4-hour solution.

headline: Australia’s New South Wales grants planning consent for 3.6GWh pumped hydro project at former coal mine
summary_80w: Gloucester Coa has received planning approval from the New South Wales government for the 3.6GWh Stratford Pumped Hydro and Solar project.

headline: Avantus progresses California solar-plus-storage projects, Fluence providing domestic content BESS
summary_80w: Avantus, with Fluence, is progressing on two solar-plus-battery energy storage system (BESS) projects in California, US.

summary_80w: Some 9.1GW/33.5GWh of large-scale BESS came online globally last month, with Saudi Arabia accounting for over one-third of the figure.

headline: Google projects are evidence that long-duration CO2 Batteries are ‘reliable capacity assets,’ Energy Dome says
summary_80w: ESN Premium speaks with Ben Potter at CO2 Battery startup Energy Dome about the company’s latest project with Google and why the market is turning in favour of long-duration energy storage (LDES).

headline: Germany ROUNDUP: TotalEnergies and Kyon, Envision and Ju:niz, Flower progress BESS portfolios
summary_80w: Energy companies are accelerating battery storage deployment in Germany, with TotalEnergies and Kyon, Envision Energy and Ju:niz, and Flower announcing significant project developments and acquisitions.

headline: What does a bankable route-to-market strategy for battery storage in Europe actually look like?
summary_80w: With technology risk now largely removed from the equation, assessing how a battery storage project will perform across market opportunities is now the main object of financiers’ scrutiny, writes Ryan Alexander of enspired.

headline: Over 4GWh of battery storage added to Australia’s updated National Renewable Energy Priority List
summary_80w: Australia's federal government has added 11 new generation and energy storage projects to its National Renewable Energy Priority List.

headline: Pumped hydro costs revised up, battery storage costs down, as Australia’s CSIRO publishes final GenCost 2025-26 report
summary_80w: Australia's CSIRO has revised pumped hydro storage costs upward in the final version of its annual GenCost electricity cost report.

headline: VIDEO: Scaling solar-coupled, distributed sodium-ion NFPP battery storage with startup Moonwatt
summary_80w: We caught up with the CCO of Netherlands-headquartered startup Moonwatt at last month's Smarter E Europe trade show in Munich for a video interview.
